title: Set network ID if available during container inspect. Fixes #24910
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Properly return NetworkID during container inspect reports for docker-compose compatibility.
```

comments.body:
I'd certainly consider this user-facing, so feel free to add a release note.

Looks like the compose tests have broken, but a cursory glance suggests it's small breaks from error message changes.

@mheon Is there any chance that the tests somehow don't use the locally built podman? The "mac address is set" failure is due to a bug in compose: https://github.com/docker/compose/pull/12439 but the other failure in compose I cannot explain (works locally *scratches head*)

> @mheon Is there any chance that the tests somehow don't use the locally built podman? The "mac address is set" failure is due to a bug in compose: [docker/compose#12439](https://github.com/docker/compose/pull/12439) but the other failure in compose I cannot explain (works locally _scratches head_)

Tests will almost certainly not use the new build podman. The packaged podman binary in $PATH is even deleted on our CI setup AFAICT so it should be impossible.

What does works locally mean? Are you actually running the tests via the test suite
`./test/compose/test-compose [test-name]`?

> What does works locally mean? Are you actually running the tests via the test suite
./test/compose/test-compose [test-name]?

Yes, let me fix up all the remaining issues and then I'll double check.

@Luap99 So I think I got most issues nailed down. https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6382311773569024/html/compose_v2-podman-fedora-41-root-host-sqlite.log.html shows the current compose output. The mac error I understand and get locally as well (compose bug, not sure what to do about it). The second error does not occur when I run it locally:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/87fa8f98-0cdf-45a9-bc36-cc74d95893ae)

And I have no idea why.  Do you have any ideas?

Also do you think that the kube tests are flaky and unrelated? Ie I am not sure what I would have done to IPC namespaces https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6663786750279680/html/int-podman-rawhide-root-host-sqlite.log.html :D

Note that the error in the compose tests:
```
[+0132s] ok 42 uptwice_idempotent - up
[+0132s] not ok 43 no container recreation in compose up (#24950)
[+0132s] #  expected:  Container uptwice_idempotent-app-1  Running
[+0132s] #    actual:  Container uptwice_idempotent-app-1  Created
[+0132s]  Container uptwice_idempotent-app-1  Starting
[+0132s]  Container uptwice_idempotent-app-1  Started
[+0133s] ok 44 uptwice_idempotent - down
```

Suggests that step 42 did not actually "up" the container in the first place.

> Also do you think that the kube tests are flaky and unrelated? 

Yes these are flakes.

I take a look at the compose test issue.


Also if you repush please change the commit message to
```
Set network ID if available during container inspect

Fixes #24910
```
This makes github properly link the issue and close it on merge. The number is meaningless in the title for a reader anyway.

The compose test failure is because we cannot use docker.io images in CI as they are just to unreliably due rate limiting, all images get redirected to quay.io/libpod via registries.conf file in CI setup. Replace the image which `quay.io/libpod/alpine` and you see the same issue.

The problem is because our alpine image is very old, the lseep command in there does not accept infinity
```
$ podman run quay.io/libpod/alpine sleep infinity
sleep: invalid number 'infinity'
```

Anyway sleep is not a command regardless because it ignore SIGTERM it makes stop take 10s which makes the test unnecessary slow. Please use top instead.
```diff
diff --git a/test/compose/uptwice_idempotent/docker-compose.yml b/test/compose/uptwice_idempotent/docker-compose.yml
index c8afeab0b2..c67283b3d3 100644
--- a/test/compose/uptwice_idempotent/docker-compose.yml
+++ b/test/compose/uptwice_idempotent/docker-compose.yml
@@ -1,4 +1,4 @@
 services:
   app:
     image: alpine
-    command: [sleep, infinity]
+    command: top
```

That leaves the mac address issue, I see you already have PR in docker-compose for that.
Thinking about this we can support the newer docker API as well if this helps. Our network backend easily support one mac per network.

> The problem is because our alpine image is very old, the lseep command in there does not accept infinity

*sigh* makes sense, sorry that I didn't think about that.

> That leaves the mac address issue, I see you already have PR in docker-compose for that. Thinking about this we can support the newer docker API as well if this helps. Our network backend easily support one mac per network.

That would obviously be great but not really in the scope of this PR (and also not sure if I could do such a large change on my own).  I sadly have no idea what the timeframe is for getting stuff into compose -- if you have backchannel connections to the devs there that would certainly help ;)

I see the PR was merged which is good, we can wait a bit. Podman 5.4-rc1 is planned in two weeks and given that such a ID field change is not a minor bug fix we should ensure it is merged for rc1.

I guess if nothing else I would be fine to temporarily skip/disable the mac test until we get a new docker-compose with the fix released.
@Luap99 Updated to the new compose release. The currently failing check is unrelated from what I can tell and the compose tests did already pass. So I guess this is good to go?

Hi @mheon & @Luap99, I am sorry to be annoying but I'd really love to get this into 5.4-rc1 and want to ensure that it doesn't fall through the cracks. Can you just shortly confirm that you will merge this in time and whether there is anything else you need me to do to get this in? Thank you very much!
@apollo13 The machine tests issues are unrelated to your changes, they are known to be flaky so I re-run them and hope this makes them pass.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *apollo13*, *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
/lgtm
Both machine jobs failed again, restarted again. Might be a real issue though. @apollo13 if this doesn't merge in a few hours, I recommend rebasing on latest main.
/lgtm
Quay flaking a lot, restarted
Mhm, still apple mac failures? I have no idea what is happening here and no mac to even try :/
Ignore them, we just keep rerunning until they pass eventually.

Thank you Matt and Paul for your reviews and helping to get this over the finish line.
